page: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based instance powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz, with no simultaneous multithreading, providing a strict 1.0 thread per core ratio. The memory configuration consists of 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows strong memory performance, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top tier. Large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaches 249.58 GB/sec, and large-block write bandwidth reaches 105.6 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core processing is also strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 17579.0 and a PassMark CPU physics score of 6741.7. However, single-core execution and integer/cryptography operations generally fall within the average range. Large LLM inference workloads, such as prompt processing for a 70B model, represent a performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 5.69 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores without the overhead of hyperthreading, which supports predictable execution times for parallel workloads. The lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must traverse the network, though this is mitigated by the 200 Gbps network baseline. The ARM64 architecture lacks x86-specific instruction sets like AVX-512 or AMX, which limits its utility for workloads compiled specifically for those vector extensions.

This instance is configured for high-performance computing tasks, parallel processing, scientific simulations, database operations, and memory-bound workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores. It is less suitable for large-scale LLM inference or workloads requiring local high-speed SSD storage.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based instance featuring an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz. Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and strong large-block memory bandwidth exceeding 249 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is also strong, though single-core execution and large LLM inference tasks perform in the average to weak ranges. Lacking x86 vector extensions like AVX-512, the instance is optimized for parallel high-performance computing, database operations, and memory-bound workloads requiring predictable, non-hyperthreaded CPU execution.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.8xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 32 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for memory-bound parallel computing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.8xlarge features 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for high-performance computing.
tagline: An ARM64-based compute instance featuring dedicated physical cores, top-tier memory bandwidth, and high-speed network connectivity for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated physical ARM64 cores with no hyperthreading
128.0 GB of RAM with top-tier memory bandwidth performance
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Strong multi-core and database benchmark results
Suited for high-performance computing, parallel processing, and database workloads
